summary: Soaring Eyes of the World and epic Other One jam dominate
blurb: The second set shines with a soaring Eyes Of The World into Wharf Rat transition and an extended The Other One jam. First‑set highlights include Cassidy, Just Like Tom Thumb's Blues and New Minglewood Blues. Bruce Hornsby's keyboards add excitement despite occasional mix muddiness.
review: Boston Garden on September 21, 1991 delivered a quintessential Saturday night show. While the first set meanders through Hell In A Bucket and settles into solid moments like Cassidy, Just Like Tom Thumb's Blues and New Minglewood Blues, it’s the second set that truly lifts the night. The band launches Uncle John's Band into a rare Saint Of Circumstance segue before exploding into an unforgettable Eyes Of The World → Wharf Rat sequence. From there the energy never wanes: a crisp, inventive jam on The Other One showcases Phil's deep bass and dynamic drumming from Mickey and Bill, capped by a powerful drum solo after Eyes Of The World. Bruce Hornsby's extroverted keyboard work fuels the excitement, even if his accordion/squeezebox occasionally muddies the early mix. Overall, this show captures the Dead at a high‑water mark of improvisational fire and tight group chemistry.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive and one is mixed, with no consistent negative comments about performance. Positive language highlights soaring Eyes Of The World, a stellar Other One jam, and strong band interplay, justifying a moderate sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Clean soundboard capture that balances the full band, highlights the strong second set, and offers the best overall listening experience despite a slight cassette‑like hiss.

key_highlights: Extended Eyes Of The World → Wharf Rat transition defining the second set
Epic The Other One jam with crisp bass and dynamic drumming
First‑set gems: Cassidy, Just Like Tom Thumb's Blues, New Minglewood Blues
Bruce Hornsby's exuberant keyboards add excitement, though his accordion can muddy early mix

band_performance.Jerry: Crisp guitar tone and lyrical delivery shine, especially during The Other One and space jams; notable improvisational choices like the follow‑up after the opening song and a fiery slipknot moment.
band_performance.Phil: Deep, crisp bass stands out on the extended Other One jam and is dead‑center in the mix throughout, providing a strong foundation.
band_performance.Bruce Hornsby: Keyboard work is extroverted and adds excitement, particularly in the second set; however, his accordion/squeezebox can dominate the mix and muddy parts of the early set.
band_performance.Mickey: Delivers a dynamic drum solo after Eyes Of The World and contributes to the early return during The Other One jam, enhancing the overall rhythmic drive.
band_performance.Billy: Provides powerful drumming alongside Mickey, with notable energy in the drum solo and throughout the second‑set improvisations.
